title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: For a $3$-graph $H$, there exists a constant $c$ depending only on $H$ such that $r(H,K_{n}^{(3)})\leq n^{c}$ for all $n$ if and only if $H$ is a subgraph of an iterated blowup of an edge.
context_text: The paper proposes a full classification of those $3$-graphs $H$ for which the off-diagonal Ramsey number $r(H,K_{n}^{(3)})$ grows polynomially with $n$. The 'if' direction (polynomial upper bound when $H$ is iterated tripartite) was already known to Erdős and Hajnal; the 'only if' direction is new. The problem was first raised explicitly by the first author at an AIM workshop in 2015.
notes: First author (Conlon) posed the problem at a 2015 AIM workshop (ref [5]); the formal conjecture as stated here appears to be the paper's own formulation.
paper_title: When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?

title: Conjecture 4.2
statement_text: If $k>s$ and $H$ is a $k$-graph which is $s$-tightly connected and not $k$-partite, then there exists a positive constant $c$ such that $r(H,K_{n}^{(k)})\geq t_{s}(n^{c})$, where the tower function $t_{i}(x)$ is defined by $t_{1}(x)=x$ and $t_{i}(x)=2^{t_{i-1}(x)}$ for all $i\geq 2$.
context_text: The authors describe this as 'likely difficult' and note it would extend Theorem 4.1 (proved in the paper). It posits a tower-height lower bound of $t_s$ for the Ramsey number of any $s$-tightly connected $k$-graph that is not $k$-partite, generalising the $k=3, s=2$ case established in Theorem 4.1.
paper_title: When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?

title: Conjecture 4.3
statement_text: If $k>s$, then there exists a positive constant $c$ such that $r(\mathcal{H}_{s}^{(k)},K_{n}^{(k)})\geq t_{s}(n^{c})$.
context_text: Here $\mathcal{H}_{s}^{(k)}$ is the family of $s$-tightly connected $k$-graphs that are not $k$-partite. This strengthens Conjecture 4.2, since $r(H,K_{n}^{(k)})\geq r(\mathcal{H}_{s}^{(k)},K_{n}^{(k)})$ for any $H\in\mathcal{H}_{s}^{(k)}$. The authors note that if true in general, this conjecture would also determine the tower height of diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers, which they describe as perhaps the central open problem in hypergraph Ramsey theory.
notes: The paper proves this conjecture for $s=2$.
paper_title: When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?
